data.narrative:
Turion Space is a Series B space & aerospace company that has raised $75M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of five public-sector peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private companies, with three direct same-stage, same-sector comparables providing the most relevant anchor points.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $75M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, yielding $142.5M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-stage Space & Aerospace companies—Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B, Aalyria at $1.3B, and Hubble Network at $400M—producing a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied, as Turion Space's revenue is not on file; the sector median of 36.9x P/S across five public tickers is noted for reference only.

Triangulating these two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $121M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median estimate of $721M. The wide spread reflects genuine uncertainty around Turion's operational maturity, customer traction, and mission success relative to its peer set. Key unknowns include revenue run-rate, contracted backlog, and technical milestones achieved since the last funding event.
